events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes hot work where the "permit scope exceeded" - meaning the work being performed goes beyond what was authorized in the hot work permit. This is a clear non-compliance issue under both NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J.

Key points:
- Hot work permit scope was exceeded - work performed outside authorized scope
- This is a serious safety violation - hot work permits are specifically scoped to control fire hazards
- NFPA 51B requires hot work permits to define the scope, area, and duration of work
- OSHA 1926 Subpart J (General Requirements) covers fire protection and prevention
- Work is at rough-complete stage

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: high - exceeding hot work permit scope creates uncontrolled fire hazards
Component conditions: non-compliant
Percent complete: ~75 (rough-complete)
Confidence: high

Let me write the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work performed beyond the authorized permit scope — work conducted outside the boundaries, activities, or duration defined in the issued hot work permit",
    "Permit scope exceeded creates uncontrolled fire and ignition hazards in areas not evaluated or protected under the original permit",
    "Fire watch, area clearance, and combustible removal requirements may not have been applied to the expanded scope of work"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all hot work immediately and secure the area",
    "Re-evaluate the actual work scope against the original permit; identify what was performed outside authorization",
    "Inspect the expanded work area for ignition sources, smoldering materials, and combustible exposure; maintain fire watch for at least 30 minutes (or per NFPA 51B) after cessation",
    "Issue a new or amended hot work permit covering the actual scope with proper area preparation, fire watch assignment, and authorization before resuming",
    "Document the permit deviation and investigate root cause of scope exceedance"
  ],
  "rationale": "Hot work permits under NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J are specifically scoped to control fire hazards by defining the location, nature, and duration of work. Exceeding the permit scope means work was performed outside the authorized fire safety controls — area inspection, combustible clearance, fire watch, and duration limits may not apply to the actual work performed. This is a direct non-compliance requiring immediate stop-work and re-permitting.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B — Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J — General Requirements (Welding and Cutting, Fire Protection)"
  ]
}
